p1.physical_facts: String of Nickels in yellow pot. Dense foliage, trailing habit.
p1.explanatory_transformations: Remained stable throughout the sequence. No significant growth or decline observed.
p1.visual_health_inference: Stable. No signs of chlorosis or wilting.

p2.physical_facts: Mexican Mint in black pot. Two primary leaves, one secondary sprout.
p2.explanatory_transformations: The sprout has shown minimal vertical movement; leaf turgor appears consistent.
p2.visual_health_inference: Stable. No necrosis or margin browning.

p3.physical_facts: Pothos in black pot. Two leaves, white rabbit anchor present.
p3.explanatory_transformations: Leaf margins show slight desiccation compared to the earliest image. The petiole angle has shifted slightly downward.
p3.visual_health_inference: Mild stress. Leaf tip necrosis is present, likely due to low humidity or substrate moisture fluctuation.

p4.physical_facts: Silver Guest in black pot. Smallest specimen, near rim.
p4.explanatory_transformations: Growth is stagnant. The specimen appears slightly more desiccated in the current image compared to T-5.
p4.visual_health_inference: Stressed. Growth is inhibited; potential root zone issues.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Soil in p3 and p4 shows white granular deposits (likely perlite or mineral salts).
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: White granular debris is present on the soil surface of p3 and p4, increasing in visibility over the sequence.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-4: Significant change in camera angle/perspective; p3 leaf tip necrosis became more pronounced.
temporal_deltas.T-4_to_T-3: Addition of white granular material to the soil surface of p3.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_T-1: Recovery of visual data; soil moisture appears lower.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: Minimal change; slight settling of soil particles.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is experiencing a period of stagnation. P3 and P4 are showing signs of environmental stress, likely related to substrate moisture or light intensity.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of white granular material on the soil surface of p3 and p4 is a recurring anomaly that may indicate salt buildup or top-dressing application.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical collection is currently in a state of maintenance. While p1 and p2 remain robust, p3 and p4 are exhibiting signs of environmental stress, specifically leaf margin necrosis and stagnant growth. The soil surface in the black pots shows evidence of mineral accumulation.
